evals.prompt: Execute this implementation plan: Plan.md is in the current directory and outlines a 5-step process to add authentication to our Express.js API using JWT tokens. Start with step 1.
evals.expected_output: Systematic execution of the plan file with checkpoint verification between steps
expectations: Output reads and confirms understanding of the plan before starting
Output executes steps sequentially, not all at once
Output verifies completion of each step before proceeding
Output pauses for user review at defined checkpoints
Output reports what was done and what comes next at each checkpoint

evals.prompt: I have a 7-step database migration plan in /docs/migration-plan.md. Execute it but pause after each step to show me what changed before proceeding. If any step fails, stop and tell me what went wrong.
evals.expected_output: Careful step-by-step execution with explicit pauses, change summaries, and error handling
expectations: Output reads the migration plan from the specified location
Output executes exactly one step at a time
Output shows what changed after each step before asking to continue
Output would stop and report clearly if a step failed
Output tracks which steps are complete and which are remaining
